narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated a 45% chance that you might fail the bar exam on your first attempt, which is above our threshold for approval. This prediction is based on your academic profile, including your Year 1 school decile of 5, which is a bit below the typical student's 5.71, and your Year 3 decile of 4, lower than the average 5.53. Your LSAT score of 28 falls well short of the dataset average of 36.7, and your undergraduate GPA of 2.7 is under the usual 3.21 seen among those who pass the bar. As a female Asian student from a middle-income family planning part-time work, your background aligns with some minorities in the data, where full-time students (92.7% of passers) and higher-income brackets tend to fare better. The model's decision was most swayed by your Year 3 decile, which nudged the prediction toward failure more than any other factor since it signals weaker performance relative to peers. Next in influence was your Year 1 decile, also pushing toward a fail prediction due to its below-average standing. Your low LSAT score ranked third, further tilting the odds against passing by highlighting a gap compared to top performers. Your racial background came fourth, slightly contributing to the risk as Asian students are less common (5.9%) among bar passers than White students (83.8%). Family income and undergraduate GPA followed, both exerting smaller pulls toward failure given their middling to low positions versus averages, while part-time status and gender had the least impact. Overall, your profile shows strengths in a stable middle-income background but lags in key academic metrics that successful bar takers typically exceed. To boost your future chances, focus on retaking the LSAT to aim above 36, strengthening your GPA through additional coursework if possible, and committing to full-time study to match the profile of most passers. Building consistently higher school deciles in any further education will also help demonstrate the upward trajectory the model favors. With targeted improvements here, you could shift your predicted odds significantly toward passing. Keep pushing—you have the potential to outperform this snapshot.
